name: Alps
description: Glacial lakes in the European Alps (Switzerland and Italy). Contains classic historical GLOF sites: Marjelensee (Aletsch, repeatedly drained during the Little Ice Age), Grubengletschersee (Saas Valley, 1968 outburst), Gornersee (Zermatt, drains annually as a jokulhlaup), and Lago del Miage (Mont Blanc, supraglacial). Smaller volumes than Himalayan lakes but excellent for monitoring research.
source: Swiss Glacier Monitoring Network (GLAMOS) + published case studies
sources: Haeberli, W., Kaab, A., Vonder Muhll, D., Teysseire, P. (2001). Prevention of outburst floods from periglacial lakes at Grubengletscher, Valais, Swiss Alps. Journal of Glaciology, 47(156), 111-122.
Huss, M., Bauder, A., Werder, M., Funk, M., Hock, R. (2007). Glacier-dammed lake outburst events of Gornersee, Switzerland. Journal of Glaciology, 53(181), 189-200.

Werder, M. A., Bauder, A., Funk, M., Keusen, H. R. (2010). Hazard assessment investigations in connection with the formation of a lake on the tongue of Unterer Grindelwaldgletscher, Bernese Alps, Switzerland. Natural Hazards and Earth System Sciences, 10, 227-237.

Diolaiuti, G., Citterio, M., Carnielli, T., D'Agata, C., Kirkbride, M., Smiraglia, C. (2006). Rates, processes and morphology of freshwater calving at Miage Glacier (Italian Alps). Hydrological Processes, 20, 2233-2244.
Mottet, G. (1962). Les laves torrentielles de la Marjelen (Suisse). Revue de geographie alpine, 50(1), 105-117. (Marjelensee 19th-century outbursts)
GLAMOS (2023). Swiss Glacier Monitoring Network annual reports. Glaciological Reports of the Cryospheric Commission of the Swiss Academy of Sciences.

Frey, H., Haeberli, W., Linsbauer, A., Huggel, C., Paul, F. (2010). A multi-level strategy for anticipating future glacier lake formation and associated hazard potentials. Natural Hazards and Earth System Sciences, 10, 339-352.
